items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Weather Whiplash Drives Natural Gas Cash Gains Across Regions
items.summary: Following a widespread retreat, physical natural gas prices gained traction Thursday, drawing support from futures as weather forecasts pointed to a mix of increased cooling and heating demand.

items.title: Futures Settle: Natural Gas Rallies Across Curve on Storage Surprise, Iran Risk Despite Seasonal Headwinds
items.summary: A larger-than-expected storage withdrawal and the looming expiration of President Trump’s five-day pause on strikes against Iran’s energy infrastructure, lifted natural gas futures, with the April contract gaining in its penultimate session as the fr

items.title: Mild Weather Undercuts LNG Demand, Even as U.S. Exports Climb
items.summary: Weather-driven LNG demand is expected to be under pressure into early April as warm temperatures settle over key markets in Europe and Asia.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Dashes of Cold Send Natural Gas Spot Prices Higher
items.summary: Next-day natural gas cash prices climbed on Thursday as near-term weather demand proved just enough in key regions.

items.title: Canadian LNG Projects Gain Appeal as Iran War Disrupts Global Natural Gas Supply
items.summary: Canadian LNG projects could potentially benefit from unrest in the Middle East that has damaged Qatar’s LNG facilities and halted vessels from transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: MidDay Futures: Natural Gas Prices Find Firm Footing Following Steep Storage Print
items.summary: Natural gas futures started lower Thursday but mustered momentum after the latest government inventory report proved bullish.

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Bullish Storage Surprise Nudges Natural Gas Futures Higher
items.summary: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) reported a 54 Bcf net withdrawal of natural gas from storage for the week ended March 20, a bullish result versus both consensus estimates and historical norms.

items.title: More U.S. Natural Gas-Fired Power Plants Proposed as Part of Japan Trade Deal
items.summary: Another natural gas-fired power plant has been proposed in Appalachia as part of a commitment announced last year by Japan to invest $550 billion in the United States.

items.title: StanChart: Europe’s Gas Prices Could Spike Above $90/MWh By The Summer
items.summary: European natural gas futures climbed toward €55/MWh on Thursday, snapping a four-day losing streak due to uncertainty over a Middle East ceasefire and ongoing threats to energy infrastructure. Initial hopes for a Middle East ceasefire quickly faded a

items.title: Washington Stress-Tests $200 Oil as War Risk Mounts
items.summary: The U.S. federal government is modeling a scenario with crude oil at $200 per barrel to see what the potential fallout of such a price surge would be for the U.S. economy. The news, reported by Bloomberg and coming from unnamed officials, comes on to

items.title: War Could Soon Force Oil Prices To Catch Up with the Massive Supply Loss
items.summary: The oil market may be sleepwalking into a significant move higher if the Strait of Hormuz remains blocked beyond March. The massive loss of supply from the Middle East has already reverberated through Asia, which depends on oil and gas from the Gulf

items.title: The Economic Stakes of a Large-Scale Attack on the National Power Grid
items.summary: The United States energy grid is in an extremely vulnerable position. Aging and underfunded, the grid is already being stressed to its limits by skyrocketing energy demand on the part of data centers as well as increasingly complicated energy flows i

items.title: North Sea Oil Fight Escalates as Starmer Cites Legal Limits
items.summary: Sir Keir Starmer has said he doesn’t hold legal powers to approve fresh exploration of North Sea oil and gas fields, with the decision falling in the hands of net zero secretary Ed Miliband. Starmer said current legislation determined that a quasi-ju

items.title: Goldman: Oil Shock Will Hit Jobs
items.summary: The oil shock will soon show up where it usually does—jobs. Goldman Sachs warned that higher crude prices could cost the U.S. labor market around 10,000 jobs per month through the rest of the year, as elevated energy costs ripple through the broader

items.title: Iran Fortifies Oil Lifeline on Kharg Island Ahead of Potential U.S. Move
items.summary: Iran has recently bolstered its defenses around Kharg Island, anticipating a possible US move to seize the key oil export hub, CNN reported this week. The island is vital to Iran’s economy, handling roughly 90% of its crude shipments, and has become
